keyDates.label: Member States establish registers of entities

regulations.name: Cyber Resilience Act
regulations.fullTitle: Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 on horizontal cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements
regulations.summary: Sets mandatory cybersecurity requirements for the design, development, production and vulnerability handling of any product with digital elements placed on the EU market. Introduces essential product requirements (Annex I Part I), vulnerability-handling requirements (Annex I Part II), CE marking, and risk-based conformity routes tied to product classes.

regulations.name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
regulations.fullTitle: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence
regulations.summary: Risk-based framework for AI systems. High-risk systems must meet requirements on risk management, data governance, technical documentation, logging, transparency, human oversight, and accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity, backed by a quality management system, conformity assessment, registration, and post-market monitoring.

regulations.name: Machinery Regulation
regulations.summary: Essential health and safety requirements for machinery, now including explicit cybersecurity provisions for safety-related control systems (protection against corruption, reliability of control systems). Requires a technical file, risk assessment, EU Declaration of Conformity, and — for high-risk categories — third-party conformity assessment.

regulations.name: IEC 62443 Series
regulations.fullTitle: IEC 62443 — Security for industrial automation and control systems (IACS)
regulations.summary: The reference standard series for OT/ICS cybersecurity. 62443-4-1 defines a secure product development lifecycle (eight practices); 62443-4-2 defines technical component requirements across seven foundational requirements. Target Security Levels (SL 1-4) scale controls to the threat. Widely used as evidence for CRA and Machinery cybersecurity obligations.

regulations.name: NIS2 Directive
regulations.fullTitle: Directive (EU) 2022/2555 on measures for a high common level of cybersecurity across the Union
regulations.summary: EU-wide cybersecurity directive imposing risk-management, governance and incident-reporting obligations on essential and important entities across critical sectors (energy, transport, water, health, digital infrastructure, manufacturing and more). Unlike the product-focused CRA, NIS2 governs the operator: management accountability, a defined set of technical and organisational measures (Art 21), and strict incident notification timelines (Art 23).

keyDates.label: Member States establish registers of entities

theme.name: Secure by Design & Default
theme.description: Building appropriate, risk-proportionate security into a product from the outset, including secure default configuration and minimised attack surface.

theme.name: Risk Management
theme.description: Establishing and maintaining a process to identify, assess and treat security and safety risks throughout the product lifecycle.

theme.name: Vulnerability Handling
theme.description: Identifying, testing for, remediating and disclosing vulnerabilities, including coordinated disclosure.

theme.name: Secure Update Management
theme.description: Providing and securely distributing security updates and patches over the product's supported lifetime.

theme.name: SBOM & Supply Chain
theme.description: Identifying and managing components and third-party dependencies, including a software bill of materials.

theme.name: Access Control
theme.description: Identification, authentication and authorization controls that prevent unauthorised access to the product and its functions.

theme.name: Data Protection & Integrity
theme.description: Protecting the confidentiality and integrity of stored, transmitted and processed data, commands and configuration.

theme.name: Logging & Monitoring
theme.description: Recording security-relevant activity and enabling detection of, and response to, security events.

theme.name: Incident Reporting
theme.description: Notifying authorities and users of actively exploited vulnerabilities and severe or serious incidents within mandated timeframes.

theme.name: Technical Documentation
theme.description: Compiling and maintaining the technical documentation / technical file that demonstrates conformity.

theme.name: Conformity Declaration
theme.description: Drawing up the EU Declaration of Conformity and applying the appropriate marking.

theme.name: Human Oversight & Transparency
theme.description: Ensuring systems are transparent to and can be effectively overseen and controlled by humans.

theme.name: Data Governance
theme.description: Governing the quality, relevance and representativeness of data used to train, validate and operate systems.

theme.name: Resilience & Availability
theme.description: Maintaining availability of essential functions and resisting denial-of-service, manipulation and other disruptions.

theme.name: Post-market Monitoring
theme.description: Actively monitoring products in the field and feeding findings back into risk management over the support period.

requirements.title: Security management
requirements.description: Establish a security management process governing the development lifecycle, including roles, responsibilities and a defined scope of the process.

requirements.title: Risk management system
requirements.description: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system running throughout the entire lifecycle of the high-risk AI system.

requirements.title: Governance and management accountability
requirements.description: The management bodies of essential and important entities shall approve the cybersecurity risk-management measures, oversee their implementation, and follow training; they can be held liable for infringements.

requirements.title: Protection against corruption
requirements.description: Machinery shall be designed and constructed so that connection to it of another device does not lead to a hazardous situation, and that hardware/software transmitting safety signals are protected against corruption.

requirements.title: Appropriate level of cybersecurity based on risk
requirements.description: Products with digital elements shall be designed, developed and produced to ensure an appropriate level of cybersecurity based on the risks.

requirements.title: Data and data governance
requirements.description: Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices appropriate to the intended purpose.

requirements.title: Third-party component management
requirements.description: Identify, assess and manage security risks of third-party and open-source components used in the product, maintaining a record of components.

requirements.title: Safety and reliability of control systems
requirements.description: Control systems shall be designed and constructed to prevent hazardous situations, withstanding intended operating stresses and external influences, including malicious attempts from third parties to create a hazardous situation.

requirements.title: No known exploitable vulnerabilities on release
requirements.description: Products shall be made available on the market without known exploitable vulnerabilities.

requirements.title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
requirements.description: Entities shall adopt policies on risk analysis and information system security as the foundation of an all-hazards, risk-based approach to protecting network and information systems.

requirements.title: Technical documentation (Annex IV)
requirements.description: Draw up technical documentation before the system is placed on the market and keep it up to date, demonstrating conformity as set out in Annex IV.

requirements.title: Incident handling
requirements.description: Entities shall implement incident-handling measures covering prevention, detection, analysis, containment, response and recovery.

requirements.title: Specification of security requirements
requirements.description: Document and maintain security requirements for the product, derived from its intended environment and threat model.

requirements.title: Secure by default configuration
requirements.description: Products shall be made available with a secure by default configuration, including the possibility to reset the product to its original state.

requirements.title: Protection of safety-related control software
requirements.description: Safety-related control software and access to it shall be protected against unintended or intentional corruption and unauthorised modification.

requirements.title: Secure by design
requirements.description: Apply secure design principles, including defence in depth and threat modelling, throughout product design.

requirements.title: Security updates
requirements.description: Ensure vulnerabilities can be addressed through security updates, including, where applicable, automatic updates and user notification.

requirements.title: Record-keeping and logging
requirements.description: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.

requirements.title: Business continuity and crisis management
requirements.description: Entities shall ensure business continuity through backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis-management arrangements.

requirements.title: Recording of intervention data
requirements.description: Evidence of intervention and of a fault, where it affects safety functions, shall be recorded to support fault detection and traceability.

requirements.title: Security verification and validation
requirements.description: Perform security verification and validation testing, including threat mitigation, vulnerability and penetration testing, before release.

requirements.title: Protection from unauthorised access
requirements.description: Protect against unauthorised access by appropriate control mechanisms, including authentication, identity and access management systems.

requirements.title: Supply chain security
requirements.description: Entities shall address security in their supply chain, including security-related aspects of the relationships between each entity and its direct suppliers or service providers.

requirements.title: Transparency and information to deployers
requirements.description: Design and develop systems so that operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret output and use it appropriately; provide instructions for use.

requirements.title: Software updates preserving safety
requirements.description: Modifications to safety-related software, including updates, shall not compromise the safety of the machinery.

requirements.title: Management of security-related issues
requirements.description: Establish a process to receive, track, assess and remediate security-related issues (defects and vulnerabilities) in the product.

requirements.title: Human oversight
requirements.description: Design and develop high-risk AI systems so that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.

requirements.title: Confidentiality of data
requirements.description: Protect the confidentiality of stored, transmitted or processed data, by encrypting relevant data at rest or in transit by state-of-the-art mechanisms.

requirements.title: Security in acquisition, development and maintenance
requirements.description: Entities shall ensure security in the acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

requirements.title: Risk assessment
requirements.description: The manufacturer shall carry out a risk assessment to determine the health and safety requirements that apply, and design and construct the machinery taking its results into account.

requirements.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
requirements.description: Design and develop systems to achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity, performing consistently throughout their lifecycle.

requirements.title: Assessing effectiveness of measures
requirements.description: Entities shall adopt policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of the cybersecurity risk-management measures they have put in place.

requirements.title: Technical file
requirements.description: Compile a technical file demonstrating that the machinery complies with the applicable essential health and safety requirements.

requirements.title: Integrity of data and configuration
requirements.description: Protect the integrity of stored, transmitted or processed data, commands, programs and configuration against unauthorised manipulation.

requirements.title: Security update management
requirements.description: Establish a process to develop, test, qualify and securely deliver security updates to product users in a timely manner.

requirements.title: Security guidelines
requirements.description: Provide documentation (security guidelines) enabling the secure integration, configuration, operation, maintenance and decommissioning of the product.

requirements.title: EU Declaration of Conformity
requirements.description: Draw up the EU declaration of conformity and affix the CE marking before placing the machinery on the market.

requirements.title: Availability of essential functions
requirements.description: Protect the availability of essential and basic functions, including resilience against and mitigation of denial-of-service attacks.

requirements.title: Resilience against manipulation
requirements.description: High-risk AI systems shall be resilient against attempts to alter their use, outputs or performance by exploiting vulnerabilities, including data poisoning and adversarial examples.

requirements.title: Basic cyber hygiene and training
requirements.description: Entities shall apply basic cyber-hygiene practices and provide cybersecurity training to staff.

requirements.title: Identification and authentication control
requirements.description: Identify and authenticate all users (humans, software processes and devices) before allowing access to the component.

requirements.title: Quality management system
requirements.description: Providers shall put in place a quality management system that ensures compliance with the Regulation, documented in a systematic and orderly manner.

requirements.title: Cryptography and encryption
requirements.description: Entities shall adopt policies and procedures on the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption.

requirements.title: Security-relevant logging
requirements.description: Provide security-related information by recording and monitoring relevant internal activity, including access to or modification of data, services or functions.

requirements.title: Identify and document components (SBOM)
requirements.description: Identify and document vulnerabilities and components, including by drawing up a software bill of materials in a commonly used machine-readable format.

requirements.title: EU declaration of conformity
requirements.description: Draw up a written EU declaration of conformity for each high-risk AI system and keep it for ten years after placing on the market.

requirements.title: Use control
requirements.description: Enforce assigned privileges to restrict use of the component to authorised actions (authorization).

requirements.title: Access control and asset management
requirements.description: Entities shall implement human-resources security, access-control policies and asset management appropriate to the risk.

requirements.title: Multi-factor authentication and secured communications
requirements.description: Entities shall use multi-factor or continuous authentication, secured voice/video/text communications, and secured emergency communication systems where appropriate.

requirements.title: System integrity
requirements.description: Protect the integrity of the component against unauthorised manipulation, including of firmware, software and information.

requirements.title: Address vulnerabilities without delay
requirements.description: In relation to the risks, address and remediate vulnerabilities without delay, including by providing security updates.

requirements.title: Post-market monitoring
requirements.description: Establish and document a post-market monitoring system that actively collects and reviews experience gained from the use of high-risk AI systems.

requirements.title: Data confidentiality
requirements.description: Protect the confidentiality of information at rest and in transit, including through cryptographic protection.

requirements.title: Incident reporting obligations
requirements.description: Entities shall notify significant incidents to the CSIRT or competent authority: an early warning within 24 hours, an incident notification within 72 hours, and a final report within one month.

requirements.title: Reporting of serious incidents
requirements.description: Report any serious incident to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States concerned, within the deadlines set out in the Regulation.

requirements.title: Regular security testing
requirements.description: Apply effective and regular tests and reviews of the security of the product with digital elements.

requirements.title: Coordinated vulnerability disclosure
requirements.description: Put in place and enforce a policy on coordinated vulnerability disclosure.

requirements.title: Timely response to events
requirements.description: Provide audit logging and the capability to respond to security violations by notifying the proper authority and reporting evidence.

requirements.title: Resource availability
requirements.description: Ensure availability of the component against the degradation or denial of essential services, including under denial-of-service conditions.

requirements.title: Secure distribution of updates
requirements.description: Ensure that mechanisms to securely distribute updates are in place to disseminate patches or updates in a timely manner.

requirements.title: Human user identification and authentication
requirements.description: The control system shall identify and authenticate all human users, and enforce authentication on all interfaces, before allowing access to the system.

requirements.title: Cybersecurity risk assessment
requirements.description: Undertake an assessment of the cybersecurity risks associated with the product and take the outcome into account during planning, design, development, production, delivery and maintenance.

requirements.title: Technical documentation
requirements.description: Draw up technical documentation containing all relevant data and details of the means used to ensure conformity, kept for at least ten years.

requirements.title: Software process and device identification
requirements.description: The control system shall identify and authenticate all software processes and devices before allowing them to access the system.

requirements.title: Authorization enforcement
requirements.description: The control system shall enforce authorizations assigned to all users and processes, restricting access to the minimum necessary for the assigned role (least privilege).

requirements.title: Report exploited vulnerabilities and severe incidents
requirements.description: Notify an actively exploited vulnerability or a severe incident: an early warning within 24 hours and a notification within 72 hours to the CSIRT and ENISA.

requirements.title: EU Declaration of Conformity
requirements.description: Draw up an EU declaration of conformity stating that the essential requirements have been fulfilled, and affix the CE marking.

requirements.title: Session lock and termination
requirements.description: The control system shall lock or terminate a session after a configurable period of inactivity or on demand, preventing further access until re-authentication.

requirements.title: Support period and updates over lifetime
requirements.description: Ensure that vulnerabilities are handled effectively during a defined support period reflecting the expected product lifetime.

requirements.title: Communication integrity
requirements.description: The control system shall protect the integrity of transmitted information, detecting unauthorised changes to communications during transmission.

requirements.title: Malicious code protection
requirements.description: The control system shall provide protection against, and detection of, malicious code at entry and exit points and on affected devices.

requirements.title: Information confidentiality
requirements.description: The control system shall protect the confidentiality of information at rest and in transit for which explicit read authorization is required, using cryptographic mechanisms where appropriate.

requirements.title: Network segmentation
requirements.description: The control system shall logically segment control networks from non-control networks and partition the system into zones and conduits according to the risk assessment.

requirements.title: Zone boundary protection
requirements.description: The control system shall monitor and control communications at zone boundaries to enforce the defined conduits, denying network traffic by default and allowing it by exception.

requirements.title: Audit log accessibility
requirements.description: The control system shall provide the capability to access audit logs on a read-only basis to authorised personnel and tools.

requirements.title: Continuous monitoring
requirements.description: The control system shall provide the capability to continuously monitor security-relevant events using commonly accepted tools and mechanisms.

requirements.title: Denial-of-service protection
requirements.description: The control system shall maintain essential functions when operating in a degraded mode as the result of a denial-of-service event.

requirements.title: Resource management and recovery
requirements.description: The control system shall limit the use of resources by security functions to prevent resource exhaustion, and support backup and recovery to a known secure state after a disruption.

sources.title: CRA Obligations — Product & Vendor Matrix
sources.description: Deep research mapping CRA product categories to real commercial products, vendors, and origins, with Class I/II/Critical notes.

sources.title: CRA Obligations (Part 2)
sources.description: Continuation of the CRA obligations research and product classification analysis.

sources.title: CRA Security Attestation (Section 7)
sources.description: Working notes on CRA security attestation and Section 7 obligations.

sources.title: CRA & SBOM — Product Flows Research
sources.description: Research on CRA-relevant product flows into and within the EU, SBOM readiness, and the notified-body bottleneck.

sources.title: CRA Hyperscale Power System — P&ID Spec
sources.description: Worked product example: a hyperscale power system P&ID specification used to illustrate CRA scoping.

sources.title: CRA Supplier Cooling Readiness
sources.description: Supplier cooling-readiness assessment used as a worked CRA supply-chain and readiness reference.

sources.title: PRD — CRA Conformity Application
sources.description: Product requirements document for the CRA conformity application: scope, personas, and the regulation knowledge & mapping engine.

sources.title: AI Act — Multi-Regulation Conformity Research
sources.description: Research on serving the AI Act alongside CRA, Machinery, and IEC 62443: shared evidence model, conformity-path selection, and notified-body orchestration.

sources.title: IEC 62443 — Data Center Zones & Tiers
sources.description: IEC 62443 zones, conduits, and tier model applied to data-center environments.

sources.title: IEC 62443 — Compliance Workshop
sources.description: Workshop material walking through IEC 62443 compliance for industrial automation and control systems.

sources.title: IEC 62443 — Deep Research
sources.description: In-depth research on the IEC 62443 series (4-1 secure development lifecycle, 4-2 component requirements).

sources.title: PRD — Digital Twin Report & Funnel
sources.description: Requirements for the digital-twin conformity report and lead funnel concept.

sources.title: CRA — Field Guide
sources.description: Comprehensive field guide to the Cyber Resilience Act: scope, product classes, obligations, conformity paths, and timelines.

sources.title: IEC 62443 — Field Guide
sources.description: Field guide to the IEC 62443 series: security levels, zones and conduits, and the 4-1/4-2 requirement families.

sources.title: EU AI Act — Field Guide
sources.description: Field guide to the EU AI Act: risk tiers, high-risk obligations, conformity assessment, and interplay with product regulations.

sources.title: Machinery Regulation — Field Guide
sources.description: Field guide to the EU Machinery Regulation: essential health & safety requirements, conformity procedures, and digital documentation.

sources.title: NIS2 — Field Guide
sources.description: Field guide to the NIS2 Directive: covered entities, risk-management measures, incident reporting, and management accountability.

sources.title: TS 50701 — Field Guide
sources.description: Field guide to CLC/TS 50701 for railway cybersecurity: applying IEC 62443 concepts to the railway domain.

sources.title: CRA CE Marking Pathways — Conformity Assessment Deep Reference
sources.description: Deep reference on CRA conformity assessment: Module A / B+C / H and EUCC pathways, Notified-Body engagement, the Annex VII technical file, the Annex V Declaration of Conformity, and the NB capacity crisis.

sources.title: CRA ↔ NIS2 Class-Designation Interlock
sources.description: How NIS2 essential-entity status drives CRA product classification (IR (EU) 2025/2392): the 'intended for use by' trigger, Class I vs Class II, the reporting interlock via ENISA, and a worked Siemens SIMATIC S7 example.

sources.title: CRA Customer Journeys
sources.description: Entry points, exposure and engagement sequences for OT product suppliers (OEMs), system integrators, and asset owners / critical-infrastructure operators approaching CRA conformity.

sources.title: CRA Preparation Service — Module A Walkthrough
sources.description: OXOT CRA service deck: a dual-perspective CRA readiness and IEC 62443 baseline assessment for equipment and OEM products — from operational IACS to a shippable product baseline meeting the CRA's 13 criteria and 8 processes.

sources.title: Data Center — TIA-942 Framework
sources.description: The TIA-942 data-center infrastructure standard: the four resilience tiers, the 2005→2022 revision history, and the site, power, cooling, cabling, fire and physical-security elements of compliance.

sources.title: TIA Family of Standards
sources.description: The TIA TR-42 hierarchy of telecommunications cabling-infrastructure standards for premises, data centers and industrial buildings — the 568 series, 569, 942-C, 1005-A, 862-C and related bulletins.

sources.title: Datacenter Supplier Status to SL-3 and SL-4
sources.description: Certification-landscape research: no data-center-native OT product holds IEC 62443-4-2 SL-3 (and SL-4 exists nowhere), the five global SL-3 components, the SL-2 frontier, and category-by-category gap analysis.
